q: The _______ viscosity is also known as absolute viscosity.
In an intake stroke of a gasoline engine, a mixture of gasoline vapour and _______ is drawn into the cylinder.
A _______ is an object that absorbs all radiation that falls on it.
The first law of thermodynamics is a statement of the conservation of _______.
In _______, heat is transferred from one place to another by the actual motion of the fluid.
The SI unit of thermal conductivity is _______.
Internal energy is the sum of the _______ and potential energies of the molecules in a system.
An _______ process occurs at a constant temperature.
In an _______ process, there is no heat transfer into or out of the system.
The _______ of a heat engine is the ratio of net work done to the heat absorbed.
Metals are generally very good _______ of heat.
A high value for thermal conductivity indicates the material is a good thermal _______.
Radiation can be transferred through a _______.
In an _______ process, the pressure of the system remains constant.
In an _______ process, the volume of the system remains constant.
A _______ engine is a device that converts heat into work.
Gasoline engines are _______ combustion engines.
The gasoline engine is also called a _______ engine.
During the _______ stroke of a gasoline engine, the spark plug ignites the mixture.
In a diesel engine, only _______ is drawn into the cylinder during the intake stroke.
A refrigerator is a heat engine operating in _______.
The Carnot cycle consists of two isothermal and two _______ processes.
Heat flux density is proportional to the magnitude of the _______ gradient.
The thermodynamics concerns the relationships between heat and other forms of _______.
The SI unit of heat is _______.
Materials that are good electric conductors are also good _______ conductors.
The _______ viscosity is the resistive flow of a fluid due to gravity.
In fluids, heat is mainly transferred by _______.
Absolute zero is equal to _______ degrees Celsius.
The work done by a gas during expansion is equal to the area under the _______ curve.
If heat is added to the system, Q is _______.
If work is done by the system, W is _______.
For a _______ engine, the work done equals heat absorbed minus heat rejected.
The _______ ratio of a diesel engine is usually between 15 and 20.
In a gasoline engine, the petrol-air mixture enters through the _______ valve.
A _______ is a perfect absorber and a perfect emitter of radiation.
Conduction is the transfer of heat through a material without any _______ motion of the material.
The surroundings of a system are everything _______ the system.
Thermal _______ is the property of a material to conduct heat.
Silver is a better thermal conductor than _______.
The entropy of an _______ system never decreases.
In an isothermal process, the change in internal energy is _______.
The intake stroke of a gasoline engine is also called the _______ stroke.
For a diesel engine, the _______ is sprayed into the cylinder by the injector.
Isothermal expansion is a process where the volume increases at constant _______.
An _______ process is one in which the volume of the system is kept constant.
The _______ engine has no spark plugs.
A system is in _______ equilibrium if its macroscopic properties do not change with time.
The transformation of heat into work is usually accomplished by _______.
The _______ law of thermodynamics defines the concept of temperature.
